query: I have a Riverpod TodoListNotifier holding a List<Todo>. Add an addTodo(Todo todo) method. The UI isn't rebuilding when I add items — fix that too.
expected_behavior: emits a NEW list via spread (`[...state, todo]`) or `.toList()`, producing a new reference each update
assigns the result back to `state` so a `!=` value triggers a rebuild
the rendering widget uses `ref.watch(...)` inside `build`, not `ref.read`
anti_behaviors: calls `state.add(todo)` mutating the existing list then re-emits the same reference
uses `ref.read(provider)` inside `build` to render state
uses legacy `StateNotifierProvider` / `ChangeNotifierProvider`

query: Add go_router to my app with a /login route and a /home route gated behind auth, plus a bottom-nav shell. Deep links must keep working.
expected_behavior: configures ONE `GoRouter` passed to `MaterialApp.router(routerConfig:)`
centralizes auth gating in the router `redirect` plus a `refreshListenable` tied to auth state
navigates with typed routes / route objects, not scattered auth checks in widgets
anti_behaviors: scatters auth checks inside individual widgets instead of the router redirect
sets a fixed `initialLocation` that clobbers an incoming deep link
creates a second router / mixes Navigator 1.0 with go_router

query: Set up a dio-based API client for my app with a Bearer auth header and request logging. Base URL is https://api.example.com.
expected_behavior: builds ONE configured `Dio` instance injected via DI, not `Dio()` per request
adds the auth Bearer token in an interceptor `onRequest`, not inline at each call site
guards `LogInterceptor` with `kDebugMode` so bodies/tokens are not logged in release
anti_behaviors: constructs `Dio()` per request instead of reusing one instance
hardcodes an API key or secret token literal in the client source
enables `LogInterceptor` unconditionally (dumps auth headers in release)

query: I need to store a few hundred structured records (notes: id, title, body, updatedAt) locally in my Flutter app, with queries and schema migrations across app updates. Recommend a package and set it up.
expected_behavior: recommends a MAINTAINED package — `drift` or `hive_ce` (or `sqflite`) — as the primary choice
defines a `schemaVersion` and an `onUpgrade` / migration path for app updates
does NOT use `SharedPreferences` as the database for the record list
anti_behaviors: recommends `Isar` (abandoned) or the classic `hive`/`Hive` (unmaintained) as the primary store
uses `SharedPreferences` to persist the structured record list
ignores migrations entirely (no schemaVersion / onUpgrade)

query: Set up dev / staging / prod build flavors for my Flutter app, each pointing at a different API base URL, readable at runtime.
expected_behavior: reads the active flavor via the official `appFlavor` constant and/or `--dart-define-from-file`
configures BOTH Android product flavors AND iOS schemes/xcconfig, not Android only
keeps real secrets out of committed `.env`/asset files (treats bundled config as non-secret)
anti_behaviors: relies on `String.fromEnvironment('FLAVOR')` as the flavor source
sets up Android flavors but omits the iOS scheme / xcconfig side
ships secrets in a bundled `.env` or asset as plaintext

query: Add Firebase Cloud Messaging to my Flutter app, including handling notifications when the app is in the background or terminated.
expected_behavior: annotates the background message handler with `@pragma('vm:entry-point')`
registers a TOP-LEVEL (not closure/anonymous) function via `FirebaseMessaging.onBackgroundMessage`
requests notification permission (Android 13+ POST_NOTIFICATIONS / iOS authorization)
anti_behaviors: omits `@pragma('vm:entry-point')` on the background handler (tree-shaken in release)
uses an inline closure as the background handler instead of a top-level function
assumes notifications display automatically with no permission request

query: Where and how should I store the user's auth token in my Flutter app? Also I want to pin my server's TLS certificate. Show the setup.
expected_behavior: stores the token in `flutter_secure_storage` (Keychain/Keystore), not SharedPreferences/plaintext
implements certificate pinning IN DART (e.g. dio/`SecurityContext`/badCertificate check), noting Flutter ignores the OS trust store
does not treat an app-bundled API key as a real secret (notes it is extractable)
anti_behaviors: stores the auth token in `SharedPreferences` or a plaintext file
sets `badCertificateCallback => true` (disabling TLS validation instead of pinning)
hardcodes a real secret/API key in Dart source as if it were secure
